title: Ports, Immutable Domain Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicitly owned TypeScript service graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Represent admitted identifiers, instants, URLs, states, and results as readonly values created only after runtime checks.
Assign every Promise a caller, awaited settlement, cancellation policy, error path, concurrency budget, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Directed Dependencies
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 9048 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove directed ports, immutable admission, Promise ownership, AbortSignal trees, and handle closure; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.

Inspect the Feed Ts Directed Dependencies trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Ts Lifecycle Ledger
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: feed-ts-lifecycle-ledger-4efys8" that uses case 3076 to prove directed ports, immutable admission, Promise ownership, AbortSignal trees, and handle closure. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, leave timers, handles, or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface, Node 24.18.0, Express 5.2.1, pg 8.22.0, fast-xml-parser 5.10.0, Zod 4.4.3, PostgreSQL 18.4, original unknown fixtures, controlled Fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, disposable databases, handle inspection, package and image review, accessibility, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
